content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 4, 1993


Barbara J. Henricks
6350 Murietta Ave., Apt. 2
Van Nuys, CA 91401

Re: Your Request for
Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-92-650

Dear Ms. Henricks:

You have requested assistance under the conflict of interest
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/
Because your request is a general inquiry, we treat it as one for
informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).2/

QUESTIONS

1. Is information concerning gifts received by Superior
Court judges available for public inspection?

2. Where are such records filed?

CONCLUSIONS

1. Superior Court judges must file periodic statements of
economic interests (Form 721) disclosing certain assets and
income, including gifts. These statements are public
documents and are available for public inspection and
reproduction.

2. Statements of economic interests filed by judges are
retained by the Fair Political Practices Commission.

_________________________
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660

Barbara J. Henricks
Page Two


ANALYSIS

The conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the Act
require public officials, including judges, to file periodic
statements of economic interests (Form 721) disclosing certain
personal assets and income, including gifts of $50 or more
received from a single source during the reporting period. The
Fair Political Practices Commission is the filing officer for
statements filed by judges.

Statements of economic interests are available for public
inspection and reproduction. (Gov. Code Section 81008.) Copies
of judges' statements of economic interests may be obtained
through the Commission at a charge of 10¢ per page. However, the
Commission may also charge a retrieval fee not to exceed five
dollars per request for copies of statements which are five or
more years old.

I hope this information has been helpful. If you have any
questions, please contact the Technical Assistance Division at
(916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Alice Hughes
Political Reform Consultant
